EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A broad surface trough across the Caribbean basin and strong high pressure across the central Atlantic maintained a moist southeasterly flow across the region. These factors combined to produced very active weather across the local region.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Flash flooding was reported at Altona vicinity Cancryn Jr. High School, Contant, Brookman Road and Harwood Highway.

CZ_NAME: ST. THOMAS / ST. JOHN AND ADJACENT ISLANDS
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Large breaking waves of heights between 10 and 15 feet produced strong rip currents, minor beach erosion and localized coastal flooding. As a result, a high surf advisory and a coastal flood advisory were in effect for the north coasts of Puerto Rico, Culebra, Vieques and the Northern U.S. Virgin Islands.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Two drowned people on Hull Bay due to high surf. The second body was found dead on Sunday March 30.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The combination of an old frontal boundary with an upper level trough produced numerous showers with thunderstorms over the U.S. Virgin Islands during the period of May 8 to May 10, 2014. Heavy rainfall produced flooding in some areas.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Brooklyn, Tutu, Lindbergh bay, Crown, Altona, Nasky areas reported flooded streets. Harwood Highway was flooded and crown mountain road had a rock slide due to heavy rains.
